heading: Chief Information Officer
label: 343 Chief Information Officer

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–458, title VIII, § 8201(a)118 Stat. 3865Dec. 17, 2004“Congress makes the following findings:“(1)Geospatial technologies and geospatial data improve government capabilities to detect, plan for, prepare for, and respond to disasters in order to save lives and protect property.“(2)Geospatial data improves the ability of information technology applications and systems to enhance public security in a cost-effective manner.“(3)Geospatial information preparedness in the United States, and specifically in the Department of Homeland Security, is insufficient because of—“(A)inadequate geospatial data compatibility;“(B)insufficient geospatial data sharing; and“(C)technology interoperability barriers.”
amendments: Amendments2004— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 108–458

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–458, title VIII, § 8201(a)118 Stat. 3865Dec. 17, 2004“Congress makes the following findings:“(1)Geospatial technologies and geospatial data improve government capabilities to detect, plan for, prepare for, and respond to disasters in order to save lives and protect property.“(2)Geospatial data improves the ability of information technology applications and systems to enhance public security in a cost-effective manner.“(3)Geospatial information preparedness in the United States, and specifically in the Department of Homeland Security, is insufficient because of—“(A)inadequate geospatial data compatibility;“(B)insufficient geospatial data sharing; and“(C)technology interoperability barriers.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Chief Information Officer shall report to the Secretary, or to another official of the Department, as the Secretary may direct.

children.heading: Geospatial information
children.label: A Geospatial information
children.text: The term “geospatial information” means graphical or digital data depicting natural or manmade physical features, phenomena, or boundaries of the earth and any information related thereto, including surveys, maps, charts, remote sensing data, and images.

children.label: i
children.text: global satellite surveillance systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: global position systems;

children.label: iii
children.text: geographic information systems;

children.label: iv
children.text: mapping equipment;

children.label: v
children.text: geocoding technology; and

children.label: vi
children.text: remote sensing devices.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Office of Geospatial Management is established within the Office of the Chief Information Officer.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: i Appointment
children.text: The Office of Geospatial Management shall be administered by the Geospatial Information Officer, who shall be appointed by the Secretary and serve under the direction of the Chief Information Officer.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: ii Functions
children.text: The Geospatial Information Officer shall assist the Chief Information Officer in carrying out all functions under this section and in coordinating the geospatial information needs of the Department.

children.label: i
children.text: providing such geospatial information as may be necessary to implement the critical infrastructure protection programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: providing leadership and coordination in meeting the geospatial information requirements of those responsible for planning, prevention, mitigation, assessment and response to emergencies, critical infrastructure protection, and other functions of the Department; and

children.label: iii
children.text: coordinating with users of geospatial information within the Department to assure interoperability and prevent unnecessary duplication.

children.label: i
children.text: coordinating the geospatial information needs and activities of the Department;

children.label: I
children.text: the Department;

children.label: II
children.text: State and local government; and

children.label: III
children.text: the private sector;

children.label: iii
children.text: coordinating with the Federal Geographic Data Committee and carrying out the responsibilities of the Department pursuant to Office of Management and Budget Circular A–16 and Executive Order 12906; and

children.label: I
children.text: fund the creation of geospatial data; and

children.label: II
children.text: execute information sharing agreements regarding geospatial data with State, local, and tribal governments.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this subsection for each fiscal year.
